meta.project: NutriLens — Barcode Resolution, OCR, AI & Monetization

product_overview: NutriLens improves barcode hit-rate using a multi-source resolution chain, device OCR fallback and AI-powered improvements; it also supports community-contributed products and a freemium monetization model with server-side scan limits.
core_goals: Maximize barcode resolution rate via a Strategy Pattern chain (community → Open Food Facts → UPC lookup) with local caching and OCR fallback.
Provide robust on-device Ingredients OCR + parser and optional AI cleanup to extract E-codes and match additives (with risk levels) for HP score calculation.
Allow community submission & verification of products (Supabase community_products) and enable barcodeless AI-recognized foods.
Add monetization: server-side daily scan limits, rewarded ads for bonus scans, and RevenueCat subscriptions for premium (ad-free, unlimited scans).
Secure AI usage via a Supabase Edge Function proxy (gemini-proxy) with rate limiting and auth validation.

features.name: Barcode Resolution Chain (ProductResolver)
features.description: Try multiple product sources in priority order (community Supabase → Open Food Facts → UPC Item DB) with timeouts; cache successful results locally and fall back to OCR when no source resolves the barcode.
user_flows: GET /rest/v1/community_products?barcode={barcode} with user JWT -> Receive 200 with full product row -> Cache product locally -> Return product to UI (happy path)
GET https://world.openfoodfacts.org/api/v0/product/{barcode}.json -> Receive 200 with OFF product -> Map OFF fields to ProductEntity -> Cache locally -> Return product to UI (source fallback)
GET https://api.upcitemdb.com/prod/trial/lookup?upc={barcode} -> Receive 200 with items (partial data: title/brand) -> Return partial product to UI and prompt OCR for ingredients (partial fallback)

GET /rest/v1/community_products?barcode={barcode} -> 200 empty/404 -> GET https://world.openfoodfacts.org/api/v0/product/{barcode}.json -> 404 -> GET https://api.upcitemdb.com/prod/trial/lookup?upc={barcode} -> 404 -> Return NotFound to repository -> UI navigates to /product/{barcode}/not-found and offer OCR/manual entry (not-found error flow)

GET /rest/v1/community_products?barcode={barcode} -> request times out -> Proceed to GET https://world.openfoodfacts.org/api/v0/product/{barcode}.json -> request times out -> GET https://api.upcitemdb.com/prod/trial/lookup?upc={barcode} -> request times out -> Return network error to UI and fallback to cached stale product if available (network error flow)

features.name: Ingredients OCR & Parser (ML Kit + Parser + Additives DB matching)
features.description: On-device ML Kit extracts raw text from an ingredients photo; parser cleans text, normalizes E-codes, matches Turkish names and E-codes against the additives DB to produce detected/unmatched additives and a confidence score.
user_flows: Process image on-device using Google ML Kit Text Recognition -> Extract raw OCR text containing ingredients (happy path step 1, local processing)

POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with body {"action":"ocr_ingredients","payload":{"text":"{rawText}"}} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 200 with cleaned ingredients text (AI-improved) -> Continue parsing

GET /rest/v1/additives?eNumber=eq.{E_CODE} (repeat per detected E-code or batch query) -> Receive 200 with additive rows including riskLevel -> Use riskLevel for HP chemical load calculation and UI chips (happy path completion)

POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with action=ocr_ingredients -> Receive 502 or 502-like AI error -> Fallback to on-device ML Kit raw text -> Run local regex E-code extraction -> If no E-codes found, return unmatchedAdditives empty and UI prompts manual entry (AI failure error flow)

GET /rest/v1/additives?eNumber=eq.{E_CODE} -> Receive 200 empty (not found) -> Treat as unmatchedAdditive (riskLevel default 3) -> Include in unmatchedAdditives and continue scoring (DB-miss error flow)

features.name: HP Score Calculator (full & partial modes)
features.description: Calculate a full HP score when product nutriments and additives are available; calculate a partial HP score using only chemical load (additives) for OCR-only or community submissions.
user_flows: GET /rest/v1/additives?eNumber=in.({E_CODES_COMMA_SEPARATED}) -> Receive 200 with additive rows and riskLevel -> HpScoreCalculator.normalizeECode + _getAdditiveRiskLevel uses DB rows -> calculateFull locally and return HpScoreResult (happy path)

GET /rest/v1/additives?eNumber=in.({E_CODES}) -> Receive 200 with some rows missing -> For missing entries assume riskLevel=3 -> calculatePartial locally (OCR-only) -> Return HpScoreResult.isPartial=true (DB-miss fallback)

GET /rest/v1/additives?eNumber=in.({E_CODES}) -> RPC or DB query fails (timeout/500) -> HpScoreCalculator._getAdditiveRiskLevel catches error and defaults to riskLevel=3 -> calculatePartial locally and return result with warning in logs (DB error fallback)

features.name: Community Product Submission & Verification
features.description: Allow authenticated users to submit OCR-derived or manual products to Supabase community_products (upsert); support reporting/verifying via product_reports and increment counters via RPCs.
user_flows: POST /rest/v1/community_products with JSON product payload and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 201/200 (upsert) -> Product available in community DB and cached locally -> UI navigates to product detail (happy path)
POST /rest/v1/community_products without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> UI prompts user to sign-in and retry submission (auth error flow)

POST /rest/v1/product_reports with {product_id, user_id, action, details} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 201 -> If action == 'verify' call POST /rpc/increment_verified_count with params {product_id_param} -> Receive 200 -> Verified_count incremented (verify happy path)

POST /rest/v1/product_reports with Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 201 but subsequent RPC increment_reported_count returns error -> Report recorded but reported_count not incremented immediately; UI shows 'report received' and logs server-side issue for later reconciliation (report RPC error flow)

features.name: AI Integration (Gemini via Supabase Edge Function gemini-proxy)
features.description: Send OCR text or image bytes to Supabase Edge Function gemini-proxy which proxies and secures calls to Gemini; used for OCR cleanup, nutrition table extraction and barcodeless food recognition.
user_flows: POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with body {"action":"ocr_ingredients","payload":{"text":"{rawText}"}} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 200 with cleaned ingredients text -> Use cleaned text for additives detection and UI (ocr_ingredients happy path)

POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with body {"action":"ocr_nutrition","payload":{"text":"{rawNutritionText}"}} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 200 with structured JSON nutrition -> Map to NutrimentsEntity and calculate full HP score locally (ocr_nutrition happy path)

POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with body {"action":"food_recognition","payload":{"image_base64":"{base64}"}} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 200 with FoodRecognitionResult JSON -> Create virtual product (ai_{ts}_{id}) via POST /rest/v1/community_products and cache locally -> UI shows AI-recognized food (food_recognition happy path)

POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 429 Rate Limit Exceeded -> UI shows 'AI rate limit reached, please try later' and falls back to ML Kit-only parsing or manual entry (rate limit error flow)

POST /functions/v1/gemini-proxy with missing GEMINI_API_KEY configured -> Edge Function returns 500 with 'Gemini API key not configured' -> UI logs error and falls back to ML Kit-only parsing (edge function config error flow)

features.name: Scan Limit Service & Monetization (server-side limits, rewarded ads, RevenueCat)
features.description: Server-side daily scan limits enforced via Supabase RPCs; free users have daily quota with optional rewarded ads granting bonus scans; premium users (RevenueCat) have unlimited scans and no ads.
user_flows: GET /api/scan_limit -> Receive 200 with list of scan limit records (public listing endpoint from backend codebase) -> Admin or diagnostics view remaining/usage (list endpoint happy path)

POST /rpc/check_and_increment_scan with body {"p_user_id":"{userId}"} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 200 {"allowed":true,"remaining":1,"is_premium":false} -> Proceed with scan (check-and-increment happy path)

POST /rpc/check_and_increment_scan with body {"p_user_id":"{userId}"} -> Receive 200 {"allowed":false,"remaining":0,"is_premium":false} -> UI opens ScanLimitSheet offering 'Watch Ad' or 'Go Premium' (limit reached flow)

POST /rpc/grant_bonus_scan with body {"p_user_id":"{userId}"} and Authorization: Bearer <user_jwt> -> Receive 200 {"granted":true,"bonus_remaining":2} after user watched rewarded ad -> Proceed with scan (rewarded ad bonus happy path)

POST /rpc/check_and_increment_scan -> RPC call fails due to network error -> Client falls back to permissive behavior (allow scan) and logs RPC error for server reconciliation (RPC network failure fallback)

features.name: Scan Limit Service
features.description: Manages user scan limits and history.
